agent.name: performance-cost-auditor
agent.role: Find N+1 queries, missing indexes, redundant API calls, oversized bundles, unnecessary re-renders, unoptimized images, and third-party cost risks for Penny.
stop_conditions_hit: services/repair Python paths not exhaustively scanned

files_skipped: services/** (FastAPI repair service — SQL and HTTP patterns not fully traced)
Image and static asset usage (no next/image audit this pass)
coverage.incomplete_reason: Preflight build failed before bundle analysis (see build artifact); no bundle-stats.txt generated.

findings.title: Supabase helpers use select('*') with no row limits
findings.description: Dashboard helpers fetch entire rows for projects, audit_runs, findings, model_usage, webhooks, and schedules. Large JSON columns (metadata, proof_hooks, history) inflate payloads and memory on the server and client. getFindings has no default LIMIT, so projects with many findings pay full-table transfer per call.

suggested_fix.approach: Replace select('*') with explicit column lists for list views; add optional limit/range parameters to getFindings and callers; consider server-side pagination for the dashboard.
steps: Identify columns required by each UI surface.
Add .select('id, title, ...') and .range() or .limit() where appropriate.

findings.title: Next.js NFT tracing warns that the whole monorepo may be pulled into the server trace
findings.description: Production build logs report an unexpected file in the NFT list, linking next.config.ts to dynamic filesystem usage (mergeEnvLocal) and API routes importing onboarding helpers. That pattern can enlarge traced server bundles and slow cold starts on Netlify/Vercel-style deploys.

proof_hooks.value: Encountered unexpected file in NFT list — import trace: next.config.ts → lib/onboarding.ts → app/api/projects/[name]/onboarding/cluster/route.ts
mergeEnvLocal reads repo root .env.local at config load

suggested_fix.approach: Scope filesystem reads to build-time only or move env merging to runtime bootstrap; narrow onboarding imports so API routes do not drag unrelated files into the trace.
steps: Review Next 16 docs on outputFileTracingRoot and turbopackIgnore hints from the build log.
Split onboarding cluster logic so the route imports a minimal module.

findings.title: next/font Google Fonts requires network at build time
findings.description: layout.tsx uses next/font/google for Inter, JetBrains Mono, and DM Serif Display. The preflight build failed because Google Fonts could not be fetched (sandboxed/offline build). CI and locked-down environments need self-hosted or committed font files to avoid flaky builds and external dependency on fonts.googleapis.com during `next build`.

proof_hooks.value: Failed to fetch Inter / JetBrains Mono / DM Serif Display from Google Fonts

suggested_fix.approach: Switch to next/font/local with files in apps/dashboard/public/fonts or use a build-time font pipeline that does not require live HTTPS to Google.
steps: Vendor font files and reference via localFont.
Document network requirements for CI if keeping next/font/google.

findings.title: Portfolio audit jobs process projects sequentially
findings.description: After resolveProjectsForJob, the worker loops `for (const project of projects)` and runs executeProjectAudit and LLM work per project. Wall-clock time and queue depth scale linearly with portfolio size; cost scales with the number of projects × audits. Parallelism exists inside passes (PASS_CONCURRENCY) but not across projects in this loop.

proof_hooks.value: for (const project of projects) { ... await executeProjectAudit(project, payload, pool);

suggested_fix.approach: If product allows, enqueue one BullMQ child job per project or cap concurrency with a semaphore to improve throughput without exceeding provider rate limits.
steps: Define max concurrent projects per portfolio job from env.
Measure Redis/Postgres load before raising concurrency.

findings.title: Project page loads the full portfolio via /api/projects
findings.description: ProjectPageClient uses usePortfolioProjects, which always GETs /api/projects and filters client-side to the current project. For users with many projects this duplicates work and payload size versus a single-project GET.

suggested_fix.approach: Add GET /api/projects/[name] as the primary load for the project route (or hydrate from server component) and keep list fetch for the portfolio index only.
steps: Compare existing /api/projects/[name] behavior with ProjectPageClient data needs.
Reduce redundant refetches after navigation between projects.

findings.title: auditWithLlm sets maxTokens to 12288 for every audit call
findings.description: The worker requests up to 12k output tokens per audit call. That raises worst-case cost per call for providers that bill by generated tokens, even when findings JSON is small. Tiered limits by audit_kind or response-size heuristics would reduce tail spend.

suggested_fix.approach: Lower default maxTokens for narrow agents; keep a higher cap only for synthesis or intelligence passes; validate with token usage metrics from model_usage.
steps: Histogram completion sizes by agent from model_usage.
Set per-audit_kind limits in routing config.

findings.title: Should findings have a composite index on (project_id, status, created_at)?
findings.description: Migrations define idx_findings_project and idx_findings_status separately. Dashboard queries often filter by project_id and optionally status with ordering by created_at. A composite index may reduce heap fetches versus BitmapAnd of two indexes, but this should be validated with real row counts and plans.

proof_hooks.value: CREATE INDEX idx_findings_project ON findings(project_id); CREATE INDEX idx_findings_status ON findings(status);
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT id, title FROM findings WHERE project_id = $1 AND status = $2 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50;

suggested_fix.approach: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on production-like data; add CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on (project_id, status, created_at DESC) if seq scans or high buffer reads appear.
steps: Capture baseline plan from Supabase SQL editor.
Compare before/after index addition in a staging branch.

next_actions.action: Stabilize dashboard production build: address next/font offline failure and NFT tracing warning, then regenerate bundle stats.
Reduce Supabase payload sizes: replace select('*') with column projections and pagination on findings.
Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE for hot findings queries; decide on composite index f-ed623fc2.
Review LLM token ceilings and portfolio job concurrency for cost/latency tradeoffs.
